verification.note: Reported by the ORACLE authors (Sankhe, Rajendran, Belgiovine, Chowdhury, Ioannidis, 'ORACLE: Optimized Radio clAssification through Convolutional neuraL nEtworks', IEEE INFOCOM 2019, arXiv:1812.01124), NOT reproduced or re-run by this repo. The 0.986 is the paper's closed-set 16-transmitter rank-1 accuracy on the SINGLE static-location experiment (Fig. 6a), raw-IQ window=128. Upgraded to 'from_paper' (2026-07-22): our canonical ORACLE closed-set split was RE-ALIGNED to the field-standard SINGLE fixed distance (8ft) -- sei-oracle-closedset-8ft-strat-tx-8010-seed42-v1, 16-tx stratified 80/10/10, 1024-windows/capture cap -- matching the paper's same-location protocol (it no longer pools all distances). Same dataset + same single-location closed-set protocol + same metric (rank1); the split-index overlap is unpublished (universal for literature rows) and ORACLE closed-set accuracy is near-invariant across distance, so 8ft is a representative same-location reference for the paper's 0.986. Distinct from a future in-repo oracle_cnn run (self_reported) on this same split. The paper's cross-distance figure (0.8713) is a separate track (see roadmap).
